entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: The crop being studied for grain mould resistance.

entities.name: GRAIN MOULD
entities.description: A disease that acts as a major constraint to sorghum production in tropical areas.

entities.name: GRAIN MOULD RESISTANCE
entities.description: The ability of sorghum genotypes to resist grain mould infection.

entities.name: TROPICAL AREAS
entities.description: Regions where grain mould is a significant constraint to sorghum production.

entities.name: BREEDING
entities.description: The process of developing improved sorghum cultivars with resistance to grain mould.

entities.name: CROSSES
entities.description: The genetic crossing of resistant sorghum genotypes to study inheritance.

entities.name: SPRINKLER IRRIGATION
entities.description: The method used to simulate conditions for evaluating grain mould reaction.

entities.name: GENERATION MEAN ANALYSIS
entities.description: A statistical method used to analyze the genetic control of grain mould reaction.

entities.name: FREQUENCY DISTRIBUTIONS
entities.description: A method used to derive the reaction of sorghum to grain mould.

entities.name: SEGREGATION RATIOS
entities.description: Calculated ratios for F2 and BC1 families to determine genetic control.

entities.name: MAJOR GENES
entities.description: Genes that primarily control the grain mould reaction in coloured sorghum.

entities.name: ADDITIVE GENE EFFECTS
entities.description: Genetic effects that contribute to the inheritance of grain mould resistance.

entities.name: DOMINANCE EFFECTS
entities.description: Genetic effects where one allele masks another in the inheritance of grain mould resistance.

entities.name: GENE INTERACTIONS
entities.description: The way different genes work together to influence grain mould resistance.

entities.name: COMPLEMENTARY RESISTANCE GENES
entities.description: Genes from different parents that together provide enhanced resistance.

entities.name: MECHANISMS OF RESISTANCE
entities.description: The biological processes that enable a plant to resist grain mould.
